amendments: A-S43-1: methodology_D added to paper_type enum
A-S43-2: IR-8 Parsimony/Modeling Principle Selection added to inference rule inventory

_patch_record.reason: Type F claim count discrepancy: AI-reader header said 4, prose contained 6 full 5-part predicates, registry had 9 entries. Ground truth established from prose. Count corrected to 6. Claim IDs renamed C-MK1.x → F-MK1.x. Crosswalk preserved in each claim object.
_patch_record.removal_reason: No corresponding prose claims found for F-MK1.7 through F-MK1.9. Presumed phantom entries from prior draft projection.

paper_title: Meta-Kaizen: A General Theory and Algorithmic Framework for the Mathematical Formalization of Self-Evolving Continuous Improvement Across Arbitrary Governance Substrates
paper_type_note: Primary contribution is the axiomatic derivation of KVS functional form (Theorem 4.1). Paper also contains application demonstrations (Section 6) and pre-registered empirical path (Section 5.4). Classified derivation_B because the derivation is the novel claim; applications are illustrations, not the object of verification. The schema enum [law_A, derivation_B, application_C] does not include 'methodology'. See paper_type_flag below.
paper_type_flag: RESOLVED S43: derivation_B confirmed for MK-P1. CBT Amendment A-S43-1 adds methodology_D to schema enum for DRP-1 and future methodology papers.

phase_ready.note: Retroactive AI layer (paper prose v8 predates DRS adoption S39). All Type F predicates finite and computable. One placeholder: F-MK1.10 pending empirical data.

falsification_syntax: I-2 5-part (FALSIFIED IF / WHERE / EVALUATION / BOUNDARY / CONTEXT)
inference_rule_inventory: IR-1 through IR-7 — Fracttalix Phase 1-2 Merged, S42, Section HR-1.1.C resolution

claim_registry.label: Axiom 1 — Weak Order
claim_registry.name: Axiom 1 — Weak Order
claim_registry.statement: For any two improvement candidates a and b, either a is at least as good as b or b is at least as good as a, and this ordering is transitive.
claim_registry.axiomatic_basis: Minimum condition for any measurement to be meaningful. Standard conjoint measurement axiom (Luce & Tukey, 1964).
claim_registry.note: Type A: taken as given. Reject only by rejecting the measurement framework itself.

claim_registry.label: Axiom 2 — Preferential Independence
claim_registry.name: Axiom 2 — Preferential Independence
claim_registry.statement: The preference ordering over candidates that differ only in component N is independent of the values of I′, C′, and T. Holds symmetrically for all four components.
claim_registry.axiomatic_basis: Luce & Tukey (1964) standard conjoint measurement starting point.
claim_registry.modeling_assumption_note: Paper explicitly flags this as a modeling assumption, not an empirical regularity. Violations require differential weights or non-additive-in-logs model. Identified as target for empirical testing in Section 5.4 and Section 7.6.

claim_registry.label: Axiom 3 — Solvability
claim_registry.name: Axiom 3 — Solvability
claim_registry.statement: For any three components held fixed, there exists a value of the fourth component that achieves any target quality score in [0,1]. Ensures scale is continuous and complete.

claim_registry.label: Axiom 4 — Archimedean Condition
claim_registry.name: Axiom 4 — Archimedean Condition
claim_registry.statement: No finite sequence of equal-interval steps in any component is unbounded. No component can be infinitely more important than another.
claim_registry.axiomatic_basis: Standard conjoint measurement axiom (Luce & Tukey, 1964; Krantz et al., 1971).

claim_registry.label: Axiom 5 — Essentialness with Veto Power
claim_registry.name: Axiom 5 — Essentialness with Veto Power
claim_registry.statement: A zero value on any single component renders the candidate inadoptable: f(0,I′,C′,T) = f(N,0,C′,T) = f(N,I′,0,T) = f(N,I′,C′,0) = 0 for all component values.
claim_registry.axiomatic_basis: Novel axiom introduced in this paper. Defense: N=0 is reimplementation; I′=0 produces no improvement; C′=0 costs overwhelm benefit; T=0 relevance window passed.
claim_registry.note: This is the axiom that eliminates the additive form and necessitates the multiplicative form. Rejecting Axiom 5 is explicitly permitted — the library implements both KVS and ADD variants.

claim_registry.label: Axiom 6 — Marginal Symmetry
claim_registry.name: Axiom 6 — Marginal Symmetry
claim_registry.statement: The marginal rate of substitution between any two components is equal at all points where their values are equal. Formally: ∂f/∂N = ∂f/∂I′ whenever N = I′; and symmetrically for all pairs.
claim_registry.axiomatic_basis: Justifies equal weights in the absence of domain-specific calibration data. Relaxing this axiom requires replacing equal exponents with estimated per-component exponents.

claim_registry.label: Governance Substrate
claim_registry.name: Governance Substrate
claim_registry.statement: A substrate S is a tuple S = (D, P, Ω, M) where D = document set, P = principal set, Ω = outcome space, M = metric function M: D × time → Ω. An improvement candidate for S is a proposed revision δ to D characterized by Novelty, Impact, Complexity, and Timeliness.

claim_registry.label: KVS Formula
claim_registry.name: KVS Formula
claim_registry.statement: KVS_j = N_j × I′_j × C′_j × T_j, where N_j, I′_j, C′_j, T_j ∈ [0,1] are defined in D-MK1.3 through D-MK1.6.
claim_registry.derivation_note: This form is derived, not stipulated. Derivation traced in F-MK1.3.

claim_registry.label: Novelty Factor
claim_registry.name: Novelty Factor
claim_registry.statement: N_j = 1 − max_{τ ∈ {t−1,t−2,t−3,t−4}} Jaccard(K_j, K_τ), where Jaccard(A,B) = |A∩B|/|A∪B|. Maximum (not mean) Jaccard similarity used. Operational target: E[N] ≥ 0.70 over rolling 4-scan window.

claim_registry.label: Normalized Impact
claim_registry.name: Normalized Impact
claim_registry.statement: I′_j = min(1, μ_j / I_max), where μ_j is the administrator's point estimate of annualized improvement and I_max is the pre-committed substrate-specific maximum. Recommended I_max values: IPS = 3.0 pp/yr; clinical = 5.0 pp; DORA = 50%; lean = 10% waste reduction.

claim_registry.label: Normalized Inverse Complexity
claim_registry.name: Normalized Inverse Complexity
claim_registry.statement: C′_j = (2.0 − C_j) / 1.0, where C_j ∈ {1.0, 1.2, 1.4, 1.6, 1.8, 2.0}. Linear rescaling maps [1.0, 2.0] bijectively onto [1.0, 0.0], fully spanning [0,1] and reaching zero at maximum complexity. Corrects prior 1/C formulation which compressed range to [1.0, 0.5].

claim_registry.label: Timeliness Score
claim_registry.name: Timeliness Score
claim_registry.statement: T_j = max(0, 1 − h_j / H_max), where h_j = months to relevance and H_max = 60 (default). H_max revisable at annual review with principal approval.

claim_registry.crosswalk_note: Renamed C-MK1.1 → F-MK1.1 per DRS type-prefix convention. Prose section 3.2.
claim_registry.label: Multiplicative functional form — uniqueness

WHERE.n_valid_alternative_forms: integer · dimensionless · count of functional forms f:[0,1]^4→[0,1] satisfying all six axioms A1–A6 AND producing at least one output differing from N×I'×C'×T

claim_registry.EVALUATION: For each axiom, state as formal constraint; check whether it admits non-multiplicative solutions. For A5, check whether any non-multiplicative f satisfies f(0,x2,x3,x4)=0 for all inputs. Finite.
claim_registry.BOUNDARY: n_valid_alternative_forms = 0 → NOT FALSIFIED. Forms identical under positive monotone transformation are not counted as alternatives.
claim_registry.CONTEXT: Uniqueness is up to positive monotone transformation. The alpha=1 exponent choice is a separate modeling decision. A counterexample must violate none of A1–A6.
claim_registry.vacuity_witness: f = N+I'+C'+T satisfies A1–A4 but fails A5: f(0,0.9,0.9,0.9)=2.7>0. Therefore n=0 for additive form → NOT FALSIFIED → non-vacuous.

WHERE.n_valid_alternative_forms: integer · dimensionless · count of functional forms f:[0,1]^4→[0,1] satisfying all six axioms A1–A6 AND producing at least one output differing from N×I'×C'×T

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each axiom, state as formal constraint; check whether it admits non-multiplicative solutions. For A5, check whether any non-multiplicative f satisfies f(0,x2,x3,x4)=0 for all inputs. Finite.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: n_valid_alternative_forms = 0 → NOT FALSIFIED. Forms identical under positive monotone transformation are not counted as alternatives.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Uniqueness is up to positive monotone transformation. The alpha=1 exponent choice is a separate modeling decision. A counterexample must violate none of A1–A6.
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: f = N+I'+C'+T satisfies A1–A4 but fails A5: f(0,0.9,0.9,0.9)=2.7>0. Therefore n=0 for additive form → NOT FALSIFIED → non-vacuous.

claim_registry.crosswalk_note: Renamed C-MK1.2 → F-MK1.2 per DRS type-prefix convention. Prose section 3.2 (Additional claims).
claim_registry.label: Equal weights derivation from Axiom A6 (Marginal Symmetry)

WHERE.n_asymmetric_weight_solutions: integer · dimensionless · count of weight vectors (w_N,w_I,w_C,w_T) with at least two unequal components satisfying (i) Marginal Symmetry A6 at (0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5) AND (ii) sum(w_i)=1

claim_registry.EVALUATION: (1) State A6 as formal constraint; (2) solve for (w_N,w_I,w_C,w_T) s.t. sum=1; (3) check for asymmetric solutions. Finite: four-variable linear system.
claim_registry.CONTEXT: Depends on A6 as stated in Section 3.2. Weakening A6 may change result.
claim_registry.vacuity_witness: (0.4,0.2,0.2,0.2) fails A6 (asymmetric marginals at symmetric point) — not a valid alternative → NOT FALSIFIED → confirms non-vacuity.
claim_registry.statement: Equal weights derivation from Axiom A6 (Marginal Symmetry)

WHERE.n_asymmetric_weight_solutions: integer · dimensionless · count of weight vectors (w_N,w_I,w_C,w_T) with at least two unequal components satisfying (i) Marginal Symmetry A6 at (0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5) AND (ii) sum(w_i)=1

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) State A6 as formal constraint; (2) solve for (w_N,w_I,w_C,w_T) s.t. sum=1; (3) check for asymmetric solutions. Finite: four-variable linear system.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Depends on A6 as stated in Section 3.2. Weakening A6 may change result.
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: (0.4,0.2,0.2,0.2) fails A6 (asymmetric marginals at symmetric point) — not a valid alternative → NOT FALSIFIED → confirms non-vacuity.

claim_registry.crosswalk_note: Renamed C-MK1.3 → F-MK1.3 per DRS type-prefix convention. Prose section 3.2 (inline claim box).
claim_registry.label: Adoption threshold κ=0.50 derivation under symmetric loss

WHERE.optimal_threshold: scalar · dimensionless · value minimizing E[Loss(κ)] under symmetric loss C_fp=C_fn and uniform Beta(1,1) prior, as specified in Section 4.7

claim_registry.EVALUATION: Define loss function with C_fp=C_fn=1.0 and Beta(1,1) prior. Compute posterior expected loss as function of κ. Find minimizer numerically or analytically. Compare to 0.50. Finite.
claim_registry.CONTEXT: Symmetric loss is an explicit simplification. The asymmetric extension κ* = C_late/(C_late+C_fp) is derived in MK-P5 Theorem 2.
claim_registry.vacuity_witness: Set C_fp=2.0, C_fn=1.0: optimal=0.333. abs(0.333-0.50)=0.167>0.001 → FALSIFIED under asymmetric loss. Confirms non-vacuity.
claim_registry.statement: Adoption threshold κ=0.50 derivation under symmetric loss

WHERE.optimal_threshold: scalar · dimensionless · value minimizing E[Loss(κ)] under symmetric loss C_fp=C_fn and uniform Beta(1,1) prior, as specified in Section 4.7

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Define loss function with C_fp=C_fn=1.0 and Beta(1,1) prior. Compute posterior expected loss as function of κ. Find minimizer numerically or analytically. Compare to 0.50. Finite.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Symmetric loss is an explicit simplification. The asymmetric extension κ* = C_late/(C_late+C_fp) is derived in MK-P5 Theorem 2.
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: Set C_fp=2.0, C_fn=1.0: optimal=0.333. abs(0.333-0.50)=0.167>0.001 → FALSIFIED under asymmetric loss. Confirms non-vacuity.

claim_registry.crosswalk_note: Renamed C-MK1.4 → F-MK1.4 per DRS type-prefix convention. Prose section 5 (inline claim box).
claim_registry.label: Simulation recall 73.8% [90% CI: 71.2, 76.2]

WHERE.recall_point_estimate: scalar · dimensionless · proportion of top-k ground-truth candidates appearing in top-k KVS-ranked candidates, averaged over n_sim=10,000 runs with distributional parameters as stated in Section 5

claim_registry.EVALUATION: Generate n_sim=10,000 candidate sets from specified Beta distributions. Rank by KVS and by noisy multiplicative ground truth. Compute recall at specified k. Compute mean and 90% CI. Check whether point estimate falls in [71.2, 76.2]. Finite.
claim_registry.BOUNDARY: recall_point_estimate in [71.2, 76.2] → NOT FALSIFIED. CI bounds inclusive.
claim_registry.CONTEXT: PLACEHOLDER PH-MK1.2: Full distributional parameters from Section 5 pending publication as standalone Channel 2 asset.
claim_registry.vacuity_witness: Recall=55.0% is logically possible if distributional parameters place most top-k KVS candidates in low ground-truth categories → FALSIFIED → non-vacuous.
claim_registry.placeholder_description: Full distributional parameters from Section 5 pending publication as standalone Channel 2 asset.

claim_registry.crosswalk_note: Renamed C-MK1.5 → F-MK1.5 per DRS type-prefix convention. Prose (Additional Type F claims).
claim_registry.label: KVS boundedness — output in [0,1] for all valid inputs

WHERE.kvs_out_of_bounds: boolean · dimensionless · true if any (N,I',C',T) in [0,1]^4 gives KVS = N×I'×C'×T outside [0,1]

claim_registry.EVALUATION: Boundary check: KVS(0,0,0,0)=0; KVS(1,1,1,1)=1. Monotone product guarantees [0,1] for all intermediate inputs. Finite.
claim_registry.CONTEXT: Analytic property. Falsifiable only by mathematical counterexample with valid input giving output outside [0,1].
claim_registry.vacuity_witness: N=−0.1 gives KVS<0 → FALSIFIED, but violates N∈[0,1]. Domain constraint is load-bearing — confirms non-vacuity.
claim_registry.statement: KVS boundedness — output in [0,1] for all valid inputs

WHERE.kvs_out_of_bounds: boolean · dimensionless · true if any (N,I',C',T) in [0,1]^4 gives KVS = N×I'×C'×T outside [0,1]

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Boundary check: KVS(0,0,0,0)=0; KVS(1,1,1,1)=1. Monotone product guarantees [0,1] for all intermediate inputs. Finite.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Analytic property. Falsifiable only by mathematical counterexample with valid input giving output outside [0,1].
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: N=−0.1 gives KVS<0 → FALSIFIED, but violates N∈[0,1]. Domain constraint is load-bearing — confirms non-vacuity.

claim_registry.crosswalk_note: Renamed C-MK1.6 → F-MK1.6 per DRS type-prefix convention. Prose (Additional Type F claims).
claim_registry.label: Self-referential applicability — no logical contradiction (Theorem 4.2)

WHERE.self_application_contradiction: boolean · dimensionless · true if applying KVS to a modification of itself produces a logical contradiction or non-termination

claim_registry.EVALUATION: (1) Specify modification M; (2) compute KVS(M) with current formula; (3) check termination and output in [0,1]; (4) check for contradiction. Finite.
claim_registry.CONTEXT: MK-P3 Appendix A demonstrates with KVS=0.413. Claim is about logical consistency, not independence of quality signal.
claim_registry.vacuity_witness: 'KVS=1 iff KVS<0.5' creates self-referential contradiction → FALSIFIED. No legitimate modification has this form. Confirms non-vacuity.

WHERE.self_application_contradiction: boolean · dimensionless · true if applying KVS to a modification of itself produces a logical contradiction or non-termination

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Specify modification M; (2) compute KVS(M) with current formula; (3) check termination and output in [0,1]; (4) check for contradiction. Finite.

channel_2_query_trace.description: Demonstration that an AI system can verify Claim F-MK1.1 (Boundedness) using this AI layer alone — zero prose references required. This trace resolves PH-DRP.2 (DRP-1 Section 7.4 live instance requirement).
channel_2_query_trace.query: Is claim F-MK1.1 (KVS Boundedness) verified for an improvement candidate with N=0.7, I′=0.6, C′=0.8, T=1.0?
channel_2_query_trace.step_3: Read FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS improvement candidate j SUCH THAT (KVS_j < 0) OR (KVS_j > 1)
channel_2_query_trace.step_4: Read WHERE: KVS_j = N_j × I′_j × C′_j × T_j per D-MK1.2
channel_2_query_trace.step_6: Check: 0.336 ∈ [0,1] — TRUE. FALSIFIED_IF condition not met.
channel_2_query_trace.step_7: Read derivation_trace: Step 1 (IR-4) + Step 2 (IR-5) — both valid for this candidate
channel_2_query_trace.result: F-MK1.1 NOT FALSIFIED for this candidate. Verification complete.

process_graph_excerpt.description: Minimal process graph entry for MK-P1 — for insertion into process-graph.json

ir_gap_register.description: Scale Normalization Condition (selection of α=1) uses a modeling principle (parsimony / face-value proportionality) that cannot be classified under IR-1 through IR-7.
ir_gap_register.proposed_resolution: Propose IR-8: Parsimony / Modeling Principle Selection — a step that selects a canonical value from a continuous family of axiom-consistent options based on a named parsimony principle. Valid iff: (a) the family of consistent options is established; (b) the parsimony principle is named and cited; (c) the selection is documented as a canonical choice, not a necessary derivation.
ir_gap_register.note: Paper already discloses this limitation in Correction 2. The gap is transparent. The AI layer flags it for governance completeness.
ir_gap_register.resolved_note: IR-8 added to inference rule inventory by CBT Amendment A-S43-2 (Session 43).

_total_claim_count_note: S53 audit complete. Counts verified: 6A + 6D + 6F = 18 total. F count corrected from 4 to 6. Claim IDs renamed C-MK1.x → F-MK1.x. Crosswalk preserved.
